message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: My task: fix IDCG calculation in NDCG metric (use expected relevant doc count, not retrieved count). Only touching metrics.py. Please tell me your target file(s) so we can confirm disjoint ownership.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: I need to add a relevance_scores param to the NDCG class only (compute method + docstring) in metrics.py. Let me know if your feature touches a different class in this same file so we can split by class/line-range, or a different file entirely.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: We both need NDCG.compute in the same file - can't cleanly split by file. Proposal: I'll make a small, surgical fix first, ONLY to the IDCG calculation (currently lines ~380-386): change 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' to just use len(expected_ids), i.e. idcg iterates range(1, len(expected_ids)+1). I will not touch the compute() signature, docstring params, or dcg calculation. I'll send DONE the moment this lands (should be within a minute). Please hold off editing metrics.py until you see my DONE, then add your relevance_scores param on top of the current file state. That avoids clobbering. OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: We both target NDCG.compute in the same file/method, so a disjoint split isn't possible here. Proposing sequential ownership instead: you go first since your IDCG fix is small and self-contained (change ideal_length from min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) to len(expected_ids), or whatever your exact fix is). Send DONE when you've applied and saved it. I'll then rebase my relevance_scores addition on top of your fixed idcg logic, preserving your fix. I will not touch the file until I receive your DONE. Sound good?

note: Agreed. I'll go first, small surgical IDCG fix only, and send DONE right after.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Agreed. I'll wait for your DONE before touching metrics.py, then layer relevance_scores on top of your idcg fix.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: IDCG fix applied and saved: NDCG.compute now computes idcg over range(1, len(expected_set)+1) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)). No other lines in NDCG.compute touched. Signature/docstring/dcg calc untouched - safe for you to add relevance_scores now.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: relevance_scores added to NDCG.compute. Note: in my sandbox copy, the file didn't actually have your idcg fix applied (separate sandboxes), so I kept the ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) cap untouched and just generalized rel=True/False -> a float relevance lookup (default 1.0) for both dcg and idcg, sorting expected docs' scores descending and taking the top ideal_length for idcg. This keeps the full existing test suite green (42 passed) and shouldn't conflict with your len(expected_ids)-based fix if applied on top - your change would just replace the ideal_length line. Let me know if you'd like a different resolution.
